description_long: This object consists of a group of industrial metal barrels arranged in neat rows, each barrel featuring a cylindrical shape with ribbed sides for structural reinforcement. The barrels display a variety of weathered colors, including black, red, yellow, and blue, with visible signs of rust, dirt, and wear, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. Each barrel has a flat, circular top with two small capped openings, typical of oil or chemical drums, and no visible handles or spouts. The surfaces are matte to slightly rough, with some barrels showing more pronounced corrosion and grime. The barrels are likely hollow inside, designed to store or transport liquids or bulk materials. Their robust construction and utilitarian appearance indicate common use in industrial, construction, or urban maintenance settings.
description: A group of weathered, ribbed metal barrels in black, red, yellow, and blue, showing signs of rust and outdoor use. Each has a flat top with two capped openings and no visible handles.
description_view_0: Front view shows the barrels' tops and color variety, with visible grime and capped openings.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the ribbed texture and cylindrical shape, with more emphasis on the blue and yellow barrels.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the tops of the barrels, focusing on the yellow and red ones, with less visible grime.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the black and gray barrels more prominently, with visible wear and rust.
